- [Field expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#field-access-expressions).\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.index\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.index "const-eval.const-expr.index")\
\
- [Array and slice indexing expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#array-and-slice-indexing-expressions), where the index is a `usize`.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.range\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.range "const-eval.const-expr.range")\
\
- [Range expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#range-expressions).\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.closure\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.closure "const-eval.const-expr.closure")\
\
- [Closure expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#closure-expressions) which don’t capture variables from the environment.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.builtin-arith-logic\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.builtin-arith-logic "const-eval.const-expr.builtin-arith-logic")\
\
- Built-in [negation](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#negation-operators), [arithmetic](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#arithmetic-and-logical-binary-operators), [logical](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#arithmetic-and-logical-binary-operators), [comparison](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#comparison-operators) or [lazy boolean](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#lazy-boolean-operators) operators used on integer and floating point types, `bool`, and `char`.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.borrows\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.borrows "const-eval.const-expr.borrows")\
\
- All forms of [borrow](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#borrow-operators) s, including raw borrows, except borrows of expressions whose temporary scopes would be extended (see [temporary lifetime extension](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-destructors.scope.lifetime-extension)) to the end of the program and which are either:\
\
\
  - Mutable borrows.\
  - Shared borrows of expressions that result in values with [interior mutability](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#interior-mutability).\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
// Due to being in tail position, this borrow extends the scope of the\
// temporary to the end of the program. Since the borrow is mutable,\
// this is not allowed in a const expression.\
const C: &u8 = &mut 0; // ERROR not allowed\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
// Const blocks are similar to initializers of `const` items.\
let _: &u8 = const { &mut 0 }; // ERROR not allowed\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
use core::sync::atomic::AtomicU8;\
// This is not allowed as 1) the temporary scope is extended to the\
// end of the program and 2) the temporary has interior mutability.\
const C: &AtomicU8 = &AtomicU8::new(0); // ERROR not allowed\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
use core::sync::atomic::AtomicU8;\
// As above.\
let _: &_ = const { &AtomicU8::new(0) }; // ERROR not allowed\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
#![allow(static_mut_refs)]\
// Even though this borrow is mutable, it's not of a temporary, so\
// this is allowed.\
const C: &u8 = unsafe { static mut S: u8 = 0; &mut S }; // OK\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
use core::sync::atomic::AtomicU8;\
// Even though this borrow is of a value with interior mutability,\
// it's not of a temporary, so this is allowed.\
const C: &AtomicU8 = {\
    static S: AtomicU8 = AtomicU8::new(0); &S // OK\
};\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
use core::sync::atomic::AtomicU8;\
// This shared borrow of an interior mutable temporary is allowed\
// because its scope is not extended.\
const C: () = { _ = &AtomicU8::new(0); }; // OK\
}\
```\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
// Even though the borrow is mutable and the temporary lives to the\
// end of the program due to promotion, this is allowed because the\
// borrow is not in tail position and so the scope of the temporary\
// is not extended via temporary lifetime extension.\
const C: () = { let _: &'static mut [u8] = &mut []; }; // OK\
//                                              ~~\
//                                     Promoted temporary.\
}\
```\
\
> Note\
>\
> In other words — to focus on what’s allowed rather than what’s not allowed — shared borrows of interior mutable data and mutable borrows are only allowed in a [const context](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#const-context) when the borrowed [place expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-expr.place-value.place-memory-location) is _transient_, _indirect_, or _static_.\
>\
> A place expression is _transient_ if it is a variable local to the current const context or an expression whose temporary scope is contained inside the current const context.\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> // The borrow is of a variable local to the initializer, therefore\
> // this place expression is transient.\
> const C: () = { let mut x = 0; _ = &mut x; };\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> // The borrow is of a temporary whose scope has not been extended,\
> // therefore this place expression is transient.\
> const C: () = { _ = &mut 0u8; };\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> // When a temporary is promoted but not lifetime extended, its\
> // place expression is still treated as transient.\
> const C: () = { let _: &'static mut [u8] = &mut []; };\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> A place expression is _indirect_ if it is a [dereference expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-expr.deref).\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> const C: () = { _ = &mut *(&mut 0); };\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> A place expression is _static_ if it is a `static` item.\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> #![allow(static_mut_refs)]\
> const C: &u8 = unsafe { static mut S: u8 = 0; &mut S };\
> }\
> ```\
\
> Note\
>\
> One surprising consequence of these rules is that we allow this,\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> const C: &[u8] = { let x: &mut [u8] = &mut []; x }; // OK\
> //                                    ~~~~~~~\
> // Empty arrays are promoted even behind mutable borrows.\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> but we disallow this similar code:\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> const C: &[u8] = &mut []; // ERROR\
> //               ~~~~~~~\
> //           Tail expression.\
> }\
> ```\
>\
> The difference between these is that, in the first, the empty array is [promoted](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#constant-promotion) but its scope does not undergo [temporary lifetime extension](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-destructors.scope.lifetime-extension), so we consider the [place expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-expr.place-value.place-memory-location) to be transient (even though after promotion the place indeed lives to the end of the program). In the second, the scope of the empty array temporary does undergo lifetime extension, and so it is rejected due to being a mutable borrow of a lifetime-extended temporary (and therefore borrowing a non-transient place expression).\
>\
> The effect is surprising because temporary lifetime extension, in this case, causes less code to compile than would without it.\
>\
> See [issue #143129](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/143129) for more details.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.deref\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.deref "const-eval.const-expr.deref")\
\
- [Dereference expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-expr.deref).\
\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
use core::cell::UnsafeCell;\
const _: u8 = unsafe {\
      let x: *mut u8 = &raw mut *&mut 0;\
      //                        ^^^^^^^\
      //             Dereference of mutable reference.\
      *x = 1; // Dereference of mutable pointer.\
      *(x as *const u8) // Dereference of constant pointer.\
};\
const _: u8 = unsafe {\
      let x = &UnsafeCell::new(0);\
      *x.get() = 1; // Mutation of interior mutable value.\
      *x.get()\
};\
}\
```\
\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.group\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.group "const-eval.const-expr.group")\
\
- [Grouped](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grouped-expressions) expressions.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.cast\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.cast "const-eval.const-expr.cast")\
\
- [Cast](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#type-cast-expressions)expressions, except\
\
  - pointer to address casts and\
  - function pointer to address casts.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.const-fn\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.const-fn "const-eval.const-expr.const-fn")\
\
- Calls of [const functions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#const-functions) and const methods.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.loop\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.loop "const-eval.const-expr.loop")\
\
- [loop](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#infinite-loops) and [while](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#predicate-loops) expressions.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-expr.if-match\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-expr.if-match "const-eval.const-expr.if-match")\
\
- [if](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#if-expressions) and [match](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#match-expressions) expressions.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context "const-eval.const-context")\
\
## [Const context](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#const-context)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.def\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.def "const-eval.const-context.def")\
\
A _const context_ is one of the following:\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.array-length\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.array-length "const-eval.const-context.array-length")\
\
- [Array type length expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#array-types)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.repeat-length\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.repeat-length "const-eval.const-context.repeat-length")\
\
- [Array repeat length expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#array-and-array-index-expressions)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.init\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.init "const-eval.const-context.init")\
\
- The initializer of\
  - [constants](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#constant-items)\
  - [statics](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#static-items)\
  - [enum discriminants](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#discriminants)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.generic\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.generic "const-eval.const-context.generic")\
\
- A [const generic argument](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#const-generics)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.block\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.block "const-eval.const-context.block")\
\
- A [const block](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#const-blocks)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-context.outer-generics\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-context.outer-generics "const-eval.const-context.outer-generics")\
\
Array type length expressions, array repeat length expressions, and const generic arguments are restricted in their use of outer generic parameters: such an expression must either be a single const generic parameter, or an expression that does not reference any generic parameters.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn "const-eval.const-fn")\
\
## [Const functions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#const-functions-1)\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.intro "const-eval.const-fn.intro")\
\
A _const function_ is a function that can be called from a const context. It is defined with the `const` qualifier, and also includes [tuple struct](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#structs) and [tuple enum variant](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#enumerations) constructors.\
\
> Example\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> const fn square(x: i32) -> i32 { x * x }\
>\
> const VALUE: i32 = square(12);\
> }\
> ```\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.const-context\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.const-context "const-eval.const-fn.const-context")\
\
When called from a const context, a const function is interpreted by the compiler at compile time. The interpretation happens in the environment of the compilation target and not the host. So `usize` is `32` bits if you are compiling against a `32` bit system, irrelevant of whether you are building on a `64` bit or a `32` bit system.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.outside-context\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.outside-context "const-eval.const-fn.outside-context")\
\
When a const function is called from outside a const context, it behaves the same as if it did not have the `const` qualifier.\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.body-restriction\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.body-restriction "const-eval.const-fn.body-restriction")\
\
The body of a const function may only use [constant expressions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#constant-expressions).\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.async\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.async "const-eval.const-fn.async")\
\
Const functions are not allowed to be [async](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#async-functions).\
\
[\[const-eval.const-fn.type-restrictions\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-const-eval.const-fn.type-restrictions "const-eval.const-fn.type-restrictions")\
\
The types of a const function’s parameters and return type are restricted to those that are compatible with a const context.\
\
[\[abi\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi "abi")\
\
# [Application binary interface (ABI)](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#application-binary-interface-abi)\
\
[\[abi.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.intro "abi.intro")\
\
This section documents features that affect the ABI of the compiled output of\
a crate.\
\
See _[extern functions](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#extern-function-qualifier)_ for information on specifying the ABI for exporting\
functions. See _[external blocks](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#external-blocks)_ for information on specifying the ABI for\
linking external libraries.\
\
[\[abi.used\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.used "abi.used")\
\
## [The `used` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-used-attribute)\
\
[\[abi.used.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.used.intro "abi.used.intro")\
\
The _`used` attribute_ can only be applied to [`static` items](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#static-items). This [attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#attributes-1) forces the\
compiler to keep the variable in the output object file (.o, .rlib, etc. excluding final binaries)\
even if the variable is not used, or referenced, by any other item in the crate.\
However, the linker is still free to remove such an item.\
\
Below is an example that shows under what conditions the compiler keeps a `static` item in the\
output object file.\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
// foo.rs\
\
// This is kept because of `#[used]`:\
#[used]\
static FOO: u32 = 0;\
\
// This is removable because it is unused:\
#[allow(dead_code)]\
static BAR: u32 = 0;\
\
// This is kept because it is publicly reachable:\
pub static BAZ: u32 = 0;\
\
// This is kept because it is referenced by a public, reachable function:\
static QUUX: u32 = 0;\
\
pub fn quux() -> &'static u32 {\
    &QUUX\
}\
\
// This is removable because it is referenced by a private, unused (dead) function:\
static CORGE: u32 = 0;\
\
#[allow(dead_code)]\
fn corge() -> &'static u32 {\
    &CORGE\
}\
}\
```\
\
```console\
\
$ rustc -O --emit=obj --crate-type=rlib foo.rs\
\
$ nm -C foo.o\
0000000000000000 R foo::BAZ\
0000000000000000 r foo::FOO\
0000000000000000 R foo::QUUX\
0000000000000000 T foo::quux\
```\
\
[\[abi.no\_mangle\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.no_mangle "abi.no_mangle")\
\
## [The `no_mangle` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-no_mangle-attribute)\
\
[\[abi.no\_mangle.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.no_mangle.intro "abi.no_mangle.intro")\
\
The _`no_mangle` attribute_ may be used on any [item](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#items) to disable standard\
symbol name mangling. The symbol for the item will be the identifier of the\
item’s name.\
\
[\[abi.no\_mangle.publicly-exported\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.no_mangle.publicly-exported "abi.no_mangle.publicly-exported")\
\
Additionally, the item will be publicly exported from the produced library or\
object file, similar to the [`used` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#the-used-attribute).\
\
[\[abi.no\_mangle.unsafe\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.no_mangle.unsafe "abi.no_mangle.unsafe")\
\
This attribute is unsafe as an unmangled symbol may collide with another symbol\
with the same name (or with a well-known symbol), leading to undefined behavior.\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
#[unsafe(no_mangle)]\
extern "C" fn foo() {}\
}\
```\
\
[\[abi.no\_mangle.edition2024\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.no_mangle.edition2024 "abi.no_mangle.edition2024")\
\
> 2024 Edition differences\
>\
> Before the 2024 edition it is allowed to use the `no_mangle` attribute without the `unsafe` qualification.\
\
[\[abi.link\_section\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section "abi.link_section")\
\
## [The `link_section` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-link_section-attribute)\
\
[\[abi.link\_section.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section.intro "abi.link_section.intro")\
\
The _`link_section` attribute_ specifies the section of the object file that a\
[function](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#functions) or [static](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#static-items)’s content will be placed into.\
\
[\[abi.link\_section.syntax\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section.syntax "abi.link_section.syntax")\
\
The `link_section` attribute uses the [MetaNameValueStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-MetaNameValueStr) syntax to specify the section name.\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
#[unsafe(no_mangle)]\
#[unsafe(link_section = ".example_section")]\
pub static VAR1: u32 = 1;\
}\
```\
\
[\[abi.link\_section.unsafe\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section.unsafe "abi.link_section.unsafe")\
\
This attribute is unsafe as it allows users to place data and code into sections\
of memory not expecting them, such as mutable data into read-only areas.\
\
[\[abi.link\_section.duplicates\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section.duplicates "abi.link_section.duplicates")\
\
Only the first use of `link_section` on an item has effect.\
\
> Note\
>\
> `rustc` lints against any use following the first with a future-compatibility warning. This may become an error in the future.\
\
[\[abi.link\_section.edition2024\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.link_section.edition2024 "abi.link_section.edition2024")\
\
> 2024 Edition differences\
>\
> Before the 2024 edition it is allowed to use the `link_section` attribute without the `unsafe` qualification.\
\
[\[abi.export\_name\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name "abi.export_name")\
\
## [The `export_name` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-export_name-attribute)\
\
[\[abi.export\_name.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name.intro "abi.export_name.intro")\
\
The _`export_name` attribute_ specifies the name of the symbol that will be\
exported on a [function](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#functions) or [static](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#static-items).\
\
[\[abi.export\_name.syntax\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name.syntax "abi.export_name.syntax")\
\
The `export_name`attribute uses the [MetaNameValueStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-MetaNameValueStr) syntax to specify the symbol name.\
\
```rust\
\
#![allow(unused)]\
fn main() {\
#[unsafe(export_name = "exported_symbol_name")]\
pub fn name_in_rust() { }\
}\
```\
\
[\[abi.export\_name.unsafe\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name.unsafe "abi.export_name.unsafe")\
\
This attribute is unsafe as a symbol with a custom name may collide with another\
symbol with the same name (or with a well-known symbol), leading to undefined\
behavior.\
\
[\[abi.export\_name.duplicates\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name.duplicates "abi.export_name.duplicates")\
\
Only the first use of `export_name` on an item has effect.\
\
> Note\
>\
> `rustc` lints against any use following the first with a future-compatibility warning. This may become an error in the future.\
\
[\[abi.export\_name.edition2024\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-abi.export_name.edition2024 "abi.export_name.edition2024")\
\
> 2024 Edition differences\
>\
> Before the 2024 edition it is allowed to use the `export_name` attribute without the `unsafe` qualification.\
\
[\[runtime\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime "runtime")\
\
# [The Rust runtime](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-rust-runtime)\
\
This section documents features that define some aspects of the Rust runtime.\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator "runtime.global_allocator")\
\
## [The `global_allocator` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-global_allocator-attribute)\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.intro "runtime.global_allocator.intro")\
\
The _`global_allocator` [attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-attributes)_ selects a [memory allocator](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/alloc/index.html).\
\
> Example\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> fn main() {\
> use core::alloc::{GlobalAlloc, Layout};\
> use std::alloc::System;\
>\
> struct MyAllocator;\
>\
> unsafe impl GlobalAlloc for MyAllocator {\
>     unsafe fn alloc(&self, layout: Layout) -> *mut u8 {\
>         unsafe { System.alloc(layout) }\
>     }\
>     unsafe fn dealloc(&self, ptr: *mut u8, layout: Layout) {\
>         unsafe { System.dealloc(ptr, layout) }\
>     }\
> }\
>\
> #[global_allocator]\
> static GLOBAL: MyAllocator = MyAllocator;\
> }\
> ```\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.syntax\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.syntax "runtime.global_allocator.syntax")\
\
The `global_allocator` attribute uses the [MetaWord](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-MetaWord) syntax.\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.allowed-positions\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.allowed-positions "runtime.global_allocator.allowed-positions")\
\
The `global_allocator` attribute may only be applied to a [static item](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#static-items) whose type implements the [`GlobalAlloc`](https://doc.rust-lang.org/core/alloc/global/trait.GlobalAlloc.html) trait.\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.duplicates\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.duplicates "runtime.global_allocator.duplicates")\
\
The `global_allocator` attribute may only be used once on an item.\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.single\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.single "runtime.global_allocator.single")\
\
The `global_allocator` attribute may only be used once in the crate graph.\
\
[\[runtime.global\_allocator.stdlib\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.global_allocator.stdlib "runtime.global_allocator.stdlib")\
\
The `global_allocator` attribute is exported from the [standard library prelude](https://doc.rust-lang.org/core/prelude/v1/index.html).\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem "runtime.windows_subsystem")\
\
## [The `windows_subsystem` attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#the-windows_subsystem-attribute)\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.intro\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.intro "runtime.windows_subsystem.intro")\
\
The _`windows_subsystem` [attribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-attributes)_ sets the [subsystem](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/fcc1zstk.aspx) when linking on a Windows target.\
\
> Example\
>\
> ```rust\
>\
> #![allow(unused)]\
> #![windows_subsystem = "windows"]\
> fn main() {\
> }\
> ```\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.syntax\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.syntax "runtime.windows_subsystem.syntax")\
\
The `windows_subsystem` attribute uses the [MetaNameValueStr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-MetaNameValueStr) syntax. Accepted values are `"console"` and `"windows"`.\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.allowed-positions\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.allowed-positions "runtime.windows_subsystem.allowed-positions")\
\
The `windows_subsystem` attribute may only be applied to the crate root.\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.duplicates\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.duplicates "runtime.windows_subsystem.duplicates")\
\
Only the first use of `windows_subsystem` has effect.\
\
> Note\
>\
> `rustc` lints against any use following the first. This may become an error in the future.\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.ignored\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.ignored "runtime.windows_subsystem.ignored")\
\
The `windows_subsystem` attribute is ignored on non-Windows targets and non-`bin` [crate types](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#linkage).\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.console\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.console "runtime.windows_subsystem.console")\
\
The `"console"` subsystem is the default. If a console process is run from an existing console then it will be attached to that console; otherwise a new console window will be created.\
\
[\[runtime.windows\_subsystem.windows\]](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-runtime.windows_subsystem.windows "runtime.windows_subsystem.windows")\
\
The `"windows"` subsystem will run detached from any existing console.\
\
> Note\
>\
> The `"windows"` subsystem is commonly used by GUI applications that do not want to display a console window on startup.\
\
# [Appendices](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#appendices)\
\
# [Grammar summary](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#grammar-summary)\
\
The following is a summary of the grammar production rules. For details on the syntax of this grammar, see _[notation.grammar.syntax](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-notation.grammar.syntax)_.\
\

\
**Syntax**\
\
[InlineAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-InlineAttribute) →\
\
inline(always)\
\
    \| inline(never)\
\
    \| inline\
\
[CollapseDebuginfoAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-CollapseDebuginfoAttribute) → collapse\_debuginfo([CollapseDebuginfoOption](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CollapseDebuginfoOption))\
\
[CollapseDebuginfoOption](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-CollapseDebuginfoOption) →\
\
yes\
\
    \| no\
\
    \| external\
\
[InnerAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-InnerAttribute) → #!\[[Attr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Attr)\]\
\
[OuterAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-OuterAttribute) → #\[[Attr](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Attr)\]\
\

## [Lexer summary](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#lexer-summary)\
\
**Lexer**\
\
[COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-COMMENT) →\
\
[LINE\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LINE_COMMENT)\
\
    \| [INNER\_LINE\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-INNER_LINE_DOC)\
\
    \| [OUTER\_LINE\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-OUTER_LINE_DOC)\
\
    \| [INNER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-INNER_BLOCK_DOC)\
\
    \| [OUTER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-OUTER_BLOCK_DOC)\
\
    \| [BLOCK\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT)\
\
[LINE\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-LINE_COMMENT) →\
\
// ( ~\[/! [LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF)\] \| // ) ~[LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF)\*\
\
    \| //[EOF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-EOF)\
\
    \| //immediately followed by LF\
\
[BLOCK\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT) →\
\
/\* ^\
\
      ( [BLOCK\_COMMENT\_OR\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT_OR_DOC) \| ( !\*/[CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CHAR) ) )\*\
\
\*/\
\
[INNER\_LINE\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-INNER_LINE_DOC) →\
\
//! ^ [LINE\_DOC\_COMMENT\_CONTENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LINE_DOC_COMMENT_CONTENT) ( [LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF) \| [EOF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-EOF) )\
\
[LINE\_DOC\_COMMENT\_CONTENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-LINE_DOC_COMMENT_CONTENT) → ( ![CR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CR) ~[LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF) )\*\
\
[INNER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-INNER_BLOCK_DOC) →\
\
/\*! ^ ( [BLOCK\_COMMENT\_OR\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT_OR_DOC) \| [BLOCK\_CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_CHAR) )\*\*/\
\
[OUTER\_LINE\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-OUTER_LINE_DOC) →\
\
/// ^ [LINE\_DOC\_COMMENT\_CONTENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LINE_DOC_COMMENT_CONTENT) ( [LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF) \| [EOF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-EOF) )\
\
[OUTER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-OUTER_BLOCK_DOC) →\
\
/\*\* !\[\*/\]\
\
      ^\
\
      ( ~\* \| [BLOCK\_COMMENT\_OR\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT_OR_DOC) )\
\
      ( [BLOCK\_COMMENT\_OR\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT_OR_DOC) \| [BLOCK\_CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_CHAR) )\*\
\
\*/\
\
[BLOCK\_CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-BLOCK_CHAR) → ( !( \*/ \| [CR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CR) ) [CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CHAR) )\
\
[BLOCK\_COMMENT\_OR\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT_OR_DOC) →\
\
[INNER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-INNER_BLOCK_DOC)\
\
    \| [OUTER\_BLOCK\_DOC](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-OUTER_BLOCK_DOC)\
\
    \| [BLOCK\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT)\
\
[IDENTIFIER\_OR\_KEYWORD](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-IDENTIFIER_OR_KEYWORD) → ( [XID\_Start](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-XID_Start) \| \_ ) [XID\_Continue](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-XID_Continue)\*\
\
[XID\_Start](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-XID_Start) → <`XID_Start` defined by Unicode>\
\
[XID\_Continue](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-XID_Continue) → <`XID_Continue` defined by Unicode>\
\
[RAW\_IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-RAW_IDENTIFIER) → r#[IDENTIFIER\_OR\_KEYWORD](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-IDENTIFIER_OR_KEYWORD)\
\
[NON\_KEYWORD\_IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-NON_KEYWORD_IDENTIFIER) → [IDENTIFIER\_OR\_KEYWORD](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-IDENTIFIER_OR_KEYWORD)except a [strict](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-lex.keywords.strict) or [reserved](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#r-lex.keywords.reserved) keyword\
\
[IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-IDENTIFIER) → [NON\_KEYWORD\_IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-NON_KEYWORD_IDENTIFIER) \| [RAW\_IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-RAW_IDENTIFIER)\
\
[RESERVED\_RAW\_IDENTIFIER](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-RESERVED_RAW_IDENTIFIER) →\
\
r# ( \_ \| crate \| self \| Self \| super ) ![XID\_Continue](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-XID_Continue)\
\
[CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-CHAR) → \[U+0000-U+D7FF U+E000-U+10FFFF\] // a Unicode scalar value\
\
[ASCII](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-ASCII) → \[U+0000-U+007F\]\
\
[NUL](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-NUL) → U+0000\
\
[EOF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-EOF) → ![CHAR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-CHAR)// End of file or input\
\
[SHEBANG](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-SHEBANG) →\
\
#! !( ( [WHITESPACE](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-WHITESPACE) \| [LINE\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LINE_COMMENT) \| [BLOCK\_COMMENT](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BLOCK_COMMENT) )\*\[ )\
\
    ~[LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF)\* ( [LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LF) \| [EOF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-EOF) )\
\

      U+0009 // Horizontal tab, `'\t'`\
\
    \| U+000A // Line feed, `'\n'`\
\
    \| U+000B // Vertical tab\
\
    \| U+000C // Form feed\
\
    \| U+000D // Carriage return, `'\r'`\
\
    \| U+0020 // Space, `' '`\
\
    \| U+0085 // Next line\
\
    \| U+200E // Left-to-right mark\
\
    \| U+200F // Right-to-left mark\
\
    \| U+2028 // Line separator\
\
    \| U+2029 // Paragraph separator\
\
[TAB](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-TAB) → U+0009 // Horizontal tab, `'\t'`\
\
[LF](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-LF) → U+000A // Line feed, `'\n'`\
\
[CR](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-CR) → U+000D // Carriage return, `'\r'`\

\

\

{\

\

## [Statements summary](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html\#statements-summary)\
\
**Syntax**\
\
[Statement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-Statement) →\
\
;\
\
    \| [Item](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Item)\
\
    \| [LetStatement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LetStatement)\
\
    \| [ExpressionStatement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-ExpressionStatement)\
\
    \| [OuterAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-OuterAttribute)\*[MacroInvocationSemi](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-MacroInvocationSemi)\
\
[LetStatement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-LetStatement) →\
\
[OuterAttribute](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-OuterAttribute)\*let[PatternNoTopAlt](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-PatternNoTopAlt) ( :[Type](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Type) )?\
\
    (\
\
=[Expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Expression)\
\
        \| =[Expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-Expression)except [LazyBooleanExpression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-LazyBooleanExpression) or end with a `}`else[BlockExpression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-BlockExpression)\
\
    )?;\
\
[ExpressionStatement](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-ExpressionStatement) →\
\
[ExpressionWithoutBlock](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-ExpressionWithoutBlock);\
\
    \| [ExpressionWithBlock](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#grammar-summary-ExpressionWithBlock);?\
\

except LazyBooleanExpression or end with a \`}\` [Expression](https://doc.rust-lang.org/reference/print.html#railroad-summary-Expression)\
